title: List SkillCheck-Free in additional skill marketplaces
description:
After 17-05-2026 (free-9xe close), SkillCheck-Free ships via vercel-labs/skills (skills.sh listing live). Goal: maximize discovery surface by listing in as many compatible marketplaces / awesome-lists / plugin directories as possible.

Investigation + listing targets:

1. Anthropic plugin marketplace — the official one. Check submission requirements (.claude-plugin/plugin.json schema, naming conventions, review process). Submit.
2. agentskills.io — referenced in original sift; verify whether it's a separate registry from vercel-labs/skills (skills.sh) or the same backend. If separate: submit.
3. mattpocock/skills — Matt accepts external skill PRs? Verify policy. If yes: PR.
4. awesome-claude-skills lists — file PRs to: github.com/olgasafonova/awesome-claude-skills (own list — add entry), other canonical awesome-claude-code lists.
5. antigravity-awesome-skills (or similar Antigravity/Cursor awesome lists) — file PRs.
6. claude-code-templates / community plugin lists — search GitHub for active community-curated lists; submit where appropriate.

Per submission, document:
- Install path users see from that marketplace (so README can link from each)
- Adoption metric (stars/forks/installs visible to that marketplace)

Deliverable: README section 'Where to find SkillCheck-Free' listing all marketplaces with install paths. Aim for >= 5 marketplaces by end of work.

Source: bimodal-adoption data from free-9xe (mattpocock 1,037 stars/skill vs glebis 2.56 vs exiao 0.12 — distribution channel matters ~400-8500x more than skill quality at install decision). 'Distribution is the new moat' — per Spiegel/Lenny 26-04-2026.

Constraints:
- SkillCheck-Free stays MIT
- Don't redistribute someone else's CLI; users invoke whichever CLI they prefer
- Each listing is additive, not exclusive — list everywhere there's a credible audience

title: Ship SkillCheck-Free skills via npm-installer pattern (mattpocock model)
description:
Adopt the npx-based skill-installer distribution pattern as the default install path for SkillCheck-Free curated reference (if/when it ships).

Source: https://github.com/mattpocock/skills (22,839 stars / 1,866 forks / MIT, 26-04-2026 sift)

Why this is P2 (positioning lever, not just convenience):
Bimodal-adoption data from 26-04-2026 sift queue across 3 third-party Claude Code skills collections:
- mattpocock: 22,839 stars / 22 skills = 1,037 stars-per-skill (recognized voice + curated + npm-installer-pattern)
- glebis: 128 stars / 50 skills = 2.56 stars-per-skill (curated, mid-recognition, no installer)
- exiao: 11 stars / 90+ skills = 0.12 stars-per-skill (personal + 'install everything' prompt-driven)

Distribution mechanism (~400-8500x ratio difference) appears to matter as much as skill quality at the adoption decision. Per Spiegel/Lenny 26-04-2026 (Brilliant): 'distribution is the new moat' — applies at skills-collection level, not just product level.

Pairs structurally with Anthropic Knowledge Work Plugins marketplace pattern (already absorbed in reading log) and Sam Henri Gold training-data-friendliness argument (item 3 same queue).

Investigation acceptance criteria:
1. Audit Matt's skills@latest npm package: what does it actually do, who maintains it, what is the security model, is it forkable / OSS / safe to depend on?
2. Compare Matt's installer to Anthropic plugin marketplace install path: is one neutral and one walled?
3. Decide: ship our own installer (skillcheck@latest), depend on Matt's, list in Anthropic marketplace, or all three?
4. If shipping own: scope npm package surface area minimally — no hidden execution, transparent fetch path, signature verification on skill content
5. License-compatibility check: SkillCheck-Free is MIT, Matt's skills are MIT, Anthropic marketplace plugins are Apache-2; what's safe to chain?

Out of scope until Cat 22 lands and SkillCheck-Free curated-reference is actually decided to ship publicly.

Sift source: 26-04-2026 mattpocock/skills reading log entry + bimodal-adoption hypothesis confirmed item 7 same queue
close_reason:
Decision settled: depend on vercel-labs/skills (the open ecosystem CLI at skills.sh). Verified working end-to-end on 17-05-2026 — npx skills add olgasafonova/SkillCheck-Free produces a clean ~30KB install (SKILL.md + references/ only) post-restructure commit 50e514f.

ACs resolution:
- AC1 (audit Matt's skills@latest npm): bypassed — vercel-labs/skills is the ecosystem CLI, not Matt's; no need to depend on Matt's specifically
- AC2 (Matt's vs Anthropic plugin marketplace comparison): not done, see follow-up bead
- AC3 (ship own / depend on Matt's / Anthropic / all three): answered — depend on vercel-labs/skills
- AC4 (if shipping own — minimal scope): N/A, not shipping own
- AC5 (license compat): SkillCheck-Free stays MIT; we don't redistribute the CLI, users invoke it

Side-question 'list in additional marketplaces too' filed as new bead — see below.
